sources.label: StockAnalysis - ATOM overview (price action: -7.6% day, -24% week, no fresh news)
GlobeNewswire - Johnson Fistel investor investigation (dated May 6, 2026, not new)
StockTitan - Atomera Q1 2026 10-Q ($11K revenue, ~$6.1M net loss, $25M raise; May filing)
Atomera IR press releases (no new releases since June 6)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict was 'remove' and nothing since June 6 changes that: today's -5.1% (and ~-24% on the week) is a flow-driven unwind of a +236% YTD speculative run, not new robotics-relevant evidence. The sell-off does not restore asymmetry here because the page thesis itself ('too indirect') is the problem, not entry price.
verdicts.bull: MST/GAA adoption progress and expanded GaN-for-RF work with Synopsys could eventually yield royalty leverage, and $41M cash post-raise removes near-term financing risk; a further washout could reset the entry for licensing-catalyst buyers.
verdicts.neutral: Q1 revenue of ~$11K against ~$6.2M opex confirms ATOM remains a pre-commercial licensing story; nothing in its pipeline is robotics-specific, so it is optionality on semiconductor licensing, not a robotics bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Still +236% YTD with essentially zero revenue, a May investor-claims investigation overhang, and a momentum crowd now exiting (-24% in a week); the unwind of a rerating without fundamentals can run much further.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A named production-license or royalty deal with a GAA or GaN manufacturer.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued momentum unwind plus dilution risk as a pre-revenue company burning ~$6M/quarter, with no robotics-specific exposure to backstop the thesis.
